title: What actually happens when you switch electricity supplier in Spain
description: Worried switching electricity supplier in Spain means engineers, outages or paperwork headaches? Here's what actually happens — and why it's simpler than you think.
excerpt: Nothing. That's the short answer. No engineer turns up. The lights don't flicker. You don't need to be home. The whole thing happens on paper, in the background.
imageAlt: Electricity meter on the wall of a Spanish home

title: Your electricity bill probably has charges on it you never agreed to
description: Spanish electricity bills often include insurance and assistance services you never signed up for. Here's where to find them, what they cost, and how to remove them.
excerpt: Pull out your last Spanish electricity bill. Find the section labelled 'Servicios y Otros Conceptos.' If there's anything listed there, read it carefully — those line items are not electricity charges.
imageAlt: Spanish electricity bill with add-on services highlighted

title: Who chose your electricity supplier in Spain?
description: Your Spanish electricity supplier was probably chosen by your lawyer, not you. Here's what that means, what to check on your bill, and how to fix it.
excerpt: When you completed on your property, did anyone ask which electricity supplier you wanted? No. They didn't. Here's what actually happened and what to do about it.
imageAlt: Spanish property purchase documents on a desk with an electricity bill

title: Why is my electricity bill high when nobody is home? Spain explained
description: Your Spanish electricity bill has two separate charges. One is for what you use. The other runs every day whether you're there or not. Here's what it is and what to do about it.
excerpt: If you've ever come back to your Spanish property after a few weeks away and found an electricity bill that seems far too high for a house that had nobody in it — you're not imagining things.
imageAlt: Empty Spanish apartment living room with an electricity bill on the side table
